**Voice 2: stepfun-flash**
- MCP is Model Context Protocol (Anthropic).
- Attestation: currently self-signed JSON schema check, no third-party verification.
- Propose: mandatory standardized attestation schema aligned with SLSA Level 3 minimum.
- Use Sigstore for signing.
- Runtime attestation: server presents short-lived X.509 cert chained to Sigstore root + real-time SBOM (SPDX 3.0) verified via in-toto attestations.
- Mandate that any server in official MCP registry must pass static SLSA 3 attestation and dynamic runtime attestation before listing, with re-attestation every 30 days or after code change.
- Testing quality: mandate standardized open-source test harness (maybe at github.com/modelcontextprotocol/test-harness? but they note it might not exist).
- Four mandatory test suites:
  1. Protocol conformance: 100% pass on all MCP 1.0 spec test cases, including edge cases (malformed tool calls, resource pagination, auth token expiry), 0 failures.
  2. Security regression tests: 100+ pre-built test cases covering OWASP Top 10 for API servers (SSRF, prompt injection via tool inputs, unauthorized resource access, data exfiltration), automated daily scans via GitHub Actions for any server with public code.
  3. Performance benchmarks: 99th percentile latency <200ms for tool calls, <100ms for resource reads under 100 concurrent requests, tested via k6 load testing scripts included in harness.
  4. Compatibility tests: pass testing against 3 latest stable releases of all official MCP client implementations (Claude Desktop, Cursor, Windsurf) with 0 compatibility failures.
- Public attestation and test results dashboard at https://mcp-attestation.anthropic.com showing real-time status, test pass rates, SBOMs, automated alerts for failures.
- Citations: SLSA spec, Sigstore docs.
